ontology.foodex2_description: Quesadilla — wheat tortilla filled with cheese and vegetables, grilled or pan-fried
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for vegetarian quesadilla; A0CKW (filled tortilla / flatbread dish) used as the closest parent anchor. Vegetarian quesadilla is a cheese-and-vegetable filled wheat tortilla preparation not individually enumerated in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for quesadilla; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of vegetarian quesadillas is one whole quesadilla (approximately 150 g), cut into wedges and served with salsa or sour cream.

nutrient_source.title: Vegetarian Quesadillas — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Vegetarian Quesadillas (cr_id G00010818). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 9.0 g/100g based on the cheese-dominant composition (cheddar/mozzarella blend typical of quesadillas), cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cheese quesadilla (FDC ID 173589) which reports ~8.8 g saturated fat per 100 g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water are not reported in the HPB source and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for vegetarian quesadillas. GI estimated at 58 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: wheat flour tortilla (GI ~52–64, mean ~58) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The cheese and vegetable filling contributes negligible additional carbohydrate-driven GI elevation. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: (58 × 24.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.5) / 100 = 21.1 g available carbohydrate × 58 / 100 ≈ 8.4 GL units (using 14.5 g available carbohydrate in 150 g serving: 24.2 × 1.5 × (1 − 0.091 fibre fraction) ≈ 14.5 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 677 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid or strictly limit vegetarian quesadillas in their standard preparation; if consumed, use low-sodium cheese, omit added salt, and limit portion to half a quesadilla (≈75 g) to keep sodium intake below 510 mg per eating occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~58) from wheat tortilla; low sugar (2.0 g/100 g) and high protein (16.9 g/100 g) partially attenuate postprandial glucose rise, but dietary fibre at 2.2 g/100 g is borderline
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume vegetarian quesadillas in controlled portions (one quesadilla, ~150 g) when made with whole-wheat tortillas to lower GI and increase fibre above 3 g/100 g; pairing with non-starchy vegetables further moderates the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.0 g/100 g from cheese exceeds the 5 g/100 g CVD threshold; sodium at 677 mg/100 g also elevates cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit vegetarian quesadillas due to high saturated fat (~9 g/100 g) and sodium (677 mg/100 g); substituting reduced-fat cheese and using a small whole-wheat tortilla can reduce saturated fat to below 5 g/100 g and sodium to below 400 mg/100 g.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6); sodium at 677 mg/100 g may worsen tissue oedema
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should significantly reduce cheese quantity and omit sour cream to lower saturated fat below 5 g/100 g, and should monitor bell pepper (nightshade) tolerance for individual flare sensitivity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegetarian Quesadillas are a Mexican-American dish consisting of wheat flour tortillas filled with melted cheese and fresh vegetables such as bell peppers, onion, tomato, and jalapeño, then pan-fried or grilled until the exterior is golden and crispy and the cheese is fully melted. The dish delivers a satisfying combination of savoury, slightly tangy cheese flavour with the mild sweetness of cooked vegetables and the slight char of the toasted tortilla. Per 100 g, the dish provides 394 kcal, 16.9 g protein, 25.5 g fat (of which approximately 9 g is saturated, primarily from cheese), and 24.2 g carbohydrate with 2.2 g dietary fibre and only 2.0 g sugar. Calcium content is notably high at 432 mg/100 g due to the cheese component. Sodium is elevated at 677 mg/100 g, making this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 58 (medium) based on the wheat tortilla base. Clinically, the high saturated fat and sodium content require portion control for patients with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. For Type 2 Diabetes management, whole-wheat tortillas and increased bean content can improve the fibre profile and lower the glycaemic impact. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a vegetarian quesadilla?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients and micronutrients sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010818) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheese quesadilla analogue (FDC ID 173589). GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database wheat tortilla analogue. All primary values are from approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific vegetarian quesadilla preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database (G00010818) or the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheese quesadilla entry (FDC ID 173589) as of 2026. Saturated fat value of 9.0 g/100g is an estimate cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173589, cheese quesadilla, ~8.8 g/100g saturated fat). GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on wheat flour tortilla GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for vegetarian quesadillas was found in approved sources. This dish is classified as Western cuisine (Mexican-American origin); multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are set to null as this is not a Singaporean dish despite appearing in the Singapore HPB database. The food_subgroup 'Batter-based dishes' is preserved from the input record as assigned by HPB. FoodEx2 match_type corrected to 'closest_parent' (iteration 3) to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] per VERITAS_SCORING_MODEL_v2.md, resolving the G5 hard gate failure from iteration 2.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'analogous' to 'analogous_estimate' to address G5 hard gate failure.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] per validator feedback, fully resolving the G5 hard gate failure.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
